body.title: Coding domain's Ollama ladder added to the cost-optimizing selector
body.text:
# D-coding-domain-hex-cloud-ladder-2026-07-01
**title:** Coding domain's Ollama ladder added to the cost-optimizing selector
**date:** 2026-07-01
**status:** open
**spawned_tickets:** T-inference-coding-ladder-hex-cloud, T-consequence-D-coding-domain-hex-cloud-ladder

## Decision narrative
Register the coding domain's Qwen/DeepSeek rungs into the registry: Hex-local qwen3-coder:30b (code) + deepseek-r1:32b (design, the first coding-domain design candidate — the rung was empty), and the Ollama-Cloud subscription flagships qwen3-coder:480b-cloud + deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud, which exist only in the cloud because they are too large for Hex's 32GB RAM. All four filed under task_class='worker' because DS always dispatches task_class='worker' and reaches harder rungs via required_difficulty (the escalation-walk override); route() filters candidates by rule.task_class BEFORE difficulty, so a designer/analyst-tier rule would be invisible to DS.

REWORDED (Akien, 2026-07-01): the intention is simply LOWEST COST WITH THE REQUIRED CAPABILITIES. An earlier draft wrongly added 'no OR/Anthropic/Google on this domain's ladder' — that is NOT the intention. Adding these rungs does not exclude anything; the selector still picks the globally cheapest CAPABLE candidate. A capable generalist on a cheaper cost_class (e.g. free-tier gemini-2.5-flash) legitimately beats this domain's subscription cloud tier — cheaper + capable is the whole rule. Classification is out of the LLM ladder entirely (compiling to graph-trees).

## Hypothesis
A coding request bumped to design-difficulty routes to the CHEAPEST capable candidate: deepseek-r1:32b/ollama when Hex is up (owned_local, $0); when Hex is down, the next cheapest capable one (a free-throttled generalist before the subscription cloud flagship); the deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud rung wins only when nothing cheaper is available.

## Measurement Signal
route(task_class='worker', domain='coding', required_difficulty='design'): Hex up -> deepseek-r1:32b/ollama; Hex down, free tier up -> gemini-2.5-flash/google_free; Hex + free both down -> deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud/ollama_cloud. Pinned by tests/inference/test_hex_registration.py.

## Intention
I intend that every request gets the lowest-cost source that has the required capabilities — the coding domain's local-Hex and cloud-subscription rungs are added as candidates so that walk has a cheap capable option at each difficulty, but nothing is excluded a priori; cost + capability decide.
